name: Pot Stickers (Vegan)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 teaspoons salt or herbal salt substitute
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine flours and warm water. Knead for 15 minutes, adding more flour to water if needed, until the dough is smooth and pliable. Cover the dough tightly with plastic wrap and let rest for 20 minutes.
2. Heat sherry in a wok over medium heat. Stir-fry onion and cabbage until limp. Add the remaining pot sticker ingredients. Cook, stirring for 2 minutes. Remove the mixture from heat and place it in a colander to drain excess moisture.
3. Divide the dough into 24 small balls. On a lightly floured board, flatten each ball of dough into a 3-4 inch circle. Fill each circle with about 1 tablespoon filling. Fold the circle into a half-moon shape and pinch the edges to seal.
4. Lightly coat two large nonstick skillets (or work in batches with one skillet) with vegetable cooking spray. Set over medium heat. When hot, add pot stickers, seam side up, flattening slightly on the bottom.
5. When the bottoms of the pot stickers are golden brown, add 1/2 cup water to each pan.
6. Cover and steam for 20 minutes.
Makes 24 pot stickers.
Per pot sticker: 4 calories, 2 grams protein, 0.7 grams fat, 8 grams carbohydrate, 0 cholesterol, 1 gram fiber
